manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: africa, button, swing, check, casino, spot, aztec, sock, model.
Your opponent's team words are: pin, fish, shoe, piano, drill, mouse, undertaker, stick.
Distractor words are: bill, dice, mine, mole, nurse, carrot, ray.
The kill words are: tube.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of our team's words: africa, button, swing, check, casino... The clue must be a single word, not any of the words in lists, nor contain parts of them. Choose to relate to several words.

Potential common theme? "Jack"? Wait not allowed because "jack" is like "jackpot" related to casino, also jack is a button (jack button?), jack swing? Not sure. Also "black" could relate to "blackjack" casino, "black button"? Not.

Maybe "swing" and "check" and "button" all have "press"? Check press? button press, press swing? Not.

Maybe "model" and "casino" and "aztec"? Not.

Could use "slot" relates to casino, button slot (like slot button?), slot swing? Not.

Maybe "dance": swing dance, African dance, check dance? Not.

Maybe "money": casino, check, button? Not.

Maybe "crown": casino, check? Not.

Potential to clue "swing", "africa", "aztec": "tribe"? Not.

Maybe "safari": Africa, check? Check pattern (checkered), but not.

Let's think of each word for possible link:

- Africa: continent, safari, desert, jungle, Nile, tribal, Afro.
- Button: fastener, press, pop, badge, shirt, remote, clothing.
- Swing: motion, pendulum, dance, baseball bat, swing set.
- Check: verify, chess piece, bank check, pattern (checkered), stop.
- Casino: gambling, blackjack, roulette, slots, chips.
- Spot: location, stain, dog spot, spot check.
- Aztec: ancient, Mexico, pyramid, civilization.
- Sock: footwear, hosiery, wool, sock puppet.
- Model: replica, fashion, prototype, showcase.

Look for common word that ties multiple: e.g., "pattern": checkered pattern, sock pattern, button pattern? Not.

"jack": button? "Jack button"? Not.

"penny": casino (penny slots), check (penny check?), not.

Maybe "strip": casino strip (Las Vegas Strip), African strip? Not.

"Vegas": casino, button? Not.

Maybe "dance": swing, African dance, maybe Aztec dance? Could.

But we need clue that is not any list word. "Dance" is not in any list. Could relate to swing, Africa (African dance), Aztec (traditional dances), maybe model (fashion show includes dancing?), not strong.

But we need to be safe from opponents' words: pin, fish, shoe, piano, drill, mouse, undertaker, stick. Also distractors: bill, dice, mine, mole, nurse, carrot, ray. Kill: tube.

Make clue ambiguous? We must ensure not accidentally hinting opponent words.

Let's try "Swing". But that's a target. can't use target as clue.

Maybe "cable": button? No.

Maybe "casino" and "check" relate to "bank". Bank check, bank casino? Not exactly.

"slot": casino slot, button slot, check slot? Not.

"chip": casino chip, button chip? Not.

"poker": casino, maybe African? Not.

But we can clue just a couple to be safe.

Maybe we clue "Africa" with "Savanna". Could also relate to "Aztec"? Not.

But the clue must relate to at least one target word but we can select multiple.

Goal: help partner guess all team words eventually, but this first clue we can give maybe covering 2-3 words.

Perhaps "fashion": model, button (fashion includes buttons), sock (fashion), maybe check (checkered pattern fashion). That's 4.

Clue "fashion" is not in lists. Does it relate to opponent words? Not obviously.

Thus:

CLUE: fashion
TARGETS: model, button, sock, check

Check if any opponent word could be associated with fashion? "shoe" is opponent; shoe is fashion too, but we risk partner guessing shoe erroneously. But clue "fashion" could lead them to guess shoe, which is opponent, causing turn to end. That's risk.

Maybe use more specific: "wardrobe": relates to model, button, sock, check (checkered shirt), maybe also "swing"? Not.

Wardrobe also could clue "shoe" still risk.

Maybe "seam": button (sewed), sock (seam), model (clothing), check (seam? maybe check seam?), but opponent words? "drill"? No.

Better: "clothing": directly ties button, sock, model (fashion model), maybe check (checkered clothing). Could cause shoe guess. Too risky.

Maybe we choose fewer words: "button" could hint "press". "Press" also relates to "check" (press check?), "model" (press model?), not.

Maybe "swing" and "check" relate to "yard"? Not.

Another approach: clue "Las" (like Las Vegas) maybe only casino. But we can just clue one word per turn.

But they ask to provide clue and list of related target words. We can choose any number, from 1 up. It's better to maximize.

Potential clue "play": swing (playground), casino (play), check (play check?), button (play button), model (play model?), maybe too many.

But "play button" is a phrase! "Play button" relates to button and casino? Not. But "play" could cause opponent "drill"? Not.

But "play" might also lead to "mouse" (play mouse?), not.

Better to pick "sound": button (sound button), casino (sound machines), swing (swing music), check? Not.

Risk of opponent "piano" which is sound instrument.

So maybe clue "mexican": Aztec, casino? Not.

Hmm.

Maybe we can do two separate clues in multiple turns, but now we only need to give one clue. Provide best possible.

I think clue "fashion" risk of shoe guess. Could we avoid shoe by making clue more specific like "buttonhole": that's a term, but includes button, maybe also sock? Not.

Clue "garment": button, sock, check (pattern), model (model wears garments). Could still guess shoe.

Maybe we can include "cassino"? No.

Alternatively, clue "slot" for casino and button (slot button), also maybe "sock" (sock slot?) not.

Clue "Vegas": casino only. But we can at least get casino.

But we could combine "African" with "Aztec" via "ancient". Clue "ancient": Aztec, Africa (ancient civilizations). Could also relate to "model"? Not. Risk opponent "drill"? Not.

Better to give two-word group: "ancient" target: aztec, africa maybe. That seems safe.

Let's produce:

CLUE: ancient
TARGETS: africa, aztec

But "ancient" could also hint "dice"? Not. Not.

Probably safe. Provide that.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'ancient'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

model, undertaker, stick, sock, shoe, africa, piano, ray, carrot, nurse, mouse, button, pin, tube, casino, fish, drill, bill, swing, check, mine, aztec, dice, spot, mole

message.reasoning:
The user asks: "Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'ancient'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer."

We need to choose up to 2 words from the given list that best relate to "ancient". The list: model, undertaker, stick, sock, shoe, africa, piano, ray, carrot, nurse, mouse, button, pin, tube, casino, fish, drill, bill, swing, check, mine, aztec, dice, spot, mole.

Which words are associated with ancient? "aztec" is an ancient civilization, so that's clearly relevant. "africa" is a continent with ancient history, but perhaps less directly. "ancient" could also relate to "mine"? Not really. So the best would be "aztec". Could also be "africa". So we can output up to 2 words. We'll choose "aztec, africa". Must start with "GUESS: ". So answer: "GUESS: aztec, africa". No extra
